rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 28, 2023 line 1. HEARING RE: SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS is continued to March 17, 2023. No later than March 10, 2023 1081-183 Mississippi Street Homeowner Association shall file a response to opposition submitted for filing on February 27, 2023 by Olusoji Fanoiki. Olusoji Fanoiki must re-submit the opposition for filing no later than 9:00 a.m. on February 28, 2023 with the correct address. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL JACOB, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. LAIT BALCHANDANI, AS TRUSTEE OF THE RAHUL BHIDE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint For Quiet Title, Declaratory Relief, And Partition By Sale
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 28, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR QUIET TITLE, DECLARATORY RELIEF, AND PARTITION BY SALE is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly indicate the person/entities with adverse claims to the title. If Plaintiff no longer claims that the estate of Bhide asserted any adverse claims to the title, Plaintiff must so clearly allege (at least by removing reference to the estate from the complaint). Motion is otherwise denied. Any challenges to the sufficiency of allegations must be made by a demurrer or a motion for judgment on the pleadings. The Court notes that the basis for Plaintiff's claim to 50% interest in the property belonging to Bhide per the Grant Deed attached to the Second Amended Complaint is not sufficiently stated (i.e. facts showing exemption from Statute of Frauds) and urges the Plaintiff to address this issue on the amendment to avoid further challenge to the pleadings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 28, 2023 line 10. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1013. rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 28, 2023 line 18. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR, First Amended Complaint on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE TIDES CENTER, VS. WILSON SEDA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 28, 2023 line 19. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Hearing will start at 9:15 a.m. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULLED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on nuisance. Plaintiff complied with all applicable requirements under the state and local law and the operative lease. =(501/ACM) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
